data.name: Binary Converter Online
data.description: Convert text to binary bytes and decode binary strings back to plain text directly in your browser. Supports UTF-8 input, space-separated byte groups, and fast local processing.
data.description_stort: Convert text into 8-bit binary groups and decode binary bytes back into readable text.
data.meta_description: Convert text to binary and decode binary bytes back with UTF-8 support.

data.title: How Binary Conversion Works
data.text:
Each byte is represented by 8 bits made of 0 and 1. Text is first converted to bytes (UTF-8), then each byte is shown as an 8-bit binary group. Decoding performs the reverse operation.

Computers store and process data using binary values represented by 0 and 1. Letters, numbers, symbols, images, and files are ultimately stored as sequences of bits. Binary conversion helps visualize how data is represented at the lowest level.

data.title: Binary and Security
data.text: Binary conversion is a representation format, not encryption. Binary data can be restored back to text without a password. Use cryptographic tools when confidentiality is required.

data.title: Common Binary Use Cases
data.text:
Binary conversion is commonly used for learning how computers store data, understanding bytes and bits, inspecting protocol payloads, studying networking concepts, and working with embedded systems or digital electronics.

Developers and students often use binary notation to visualize character encoding, troubleshoot byte-level data, and better understand how information is represented inside computers.

data.title: Binary vs Hex
data.text:
Binary displays every bit using only 0 and 1, providing the most detailed view of data representation. However, long binary sequences can be difficult to read.

Hexadecimal is a more compact representation where each hex digit represents four binary bits. Binary is often preferred for learning and bit-level analysis, while Hex is commonly used for debugging and inspecting raw data.

data.question: Is binary conversion encryption?
data.answer: No. It only changes representation into bits and is reversible.

data.question: Can I decode binary with spaces?
data.answer: Yes. Space-separated 8-bit groups like 01001000 01101001 are supported.

data.question: Why does binary output look longer?
data.answer: Each byte becomes 8 symbols, so binary representation is much longer than plain text.

data.question: Which symbols are valid in binary input?
data.answer: Only 0, 1, and separators like spaces between byte groups.

data.question: Why can binary decoding fail?
data.answer: Usually because input contains invalid characters or malformed byte groups.

data.question: Why are binary groups usually 8 bits long?
data.answer: Eight bits form one byte, which is the standard unit used to represent characters and data in modern computing systems.

data.question: What is the difference between Binary and Hex?
data.answer: Binary shows every bit using 0 and 1, while Hex groups bits into a shorter hexadecimal representation. Hex is easier to read, while Binary shows the exact bit structure.

data.question: Can binary represent Unicode characters?
data.answer: Yes. Unicode text is first encoded into bytes, usually using UTF-8, and those bytes can then be represented as binary values.

data.title: Encode plain text
data.description: Convert readable text into 8-bit binary byte groups.

data.title: Decode binary bytes
data.description: Restore readable text from binary byte sequences.

data.title: Encode JSON payload
data.description: Inspect JSON data at the bit level using binary notation.

data.title: Encode Unicode text
data.description: Binary preserves UTF-8 byte sequences, accented characters, and emoji.

Пустые строки допустимы: при импорте это удалит перевод для конкретного языка.
Для добавления новых элементов в sections blocks/faq/examples/tags создавайте объект без id или с id=0: импорт создаст запись автоматически.
Новые элементы (без id / с id=0) разрешено добавлять только в файле, где meta.language == meta.default_language.
